claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have one child. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! To help determine your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your situation.

Could you please tell me your child's name and age?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex and they're 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask an important question:

Is Alex currently in approved education or training? (For example, attending sixth form, college, or an approved training course)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school but aren't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment:

Does Alex currently work, and if so, how many hours per week do they work?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A couple more questions:

Do you currently live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one final question:

Does Alex receive any benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: While you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, the rules are different for older children. Since Alex is 18 years old, you would only continue to qualify for Child Benefit if Alex is under 20 **and** stays in approved education or training.

Because Alex has finished school and is not currently in any education or training, the eligibility criteria are not met.

**Please note:** If Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future (such as college, sixth form, or an approved training course), you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit again at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in any approved education or training, the eligibility criteria are not met. Alex does not work 24 hours or more per week and does not receive benefits in their own right, but the key issue is that they are over 16 and not in approved education or training, which is required for continued Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old child Alex. While the claimant meets the basic residency requirements and lives with their child, Alex is over the age of 16 and is not currently in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility for children aged 16-19 requires that they remain in approved education or training. Since this condition is not met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in any approved education or training, the eligibility criteria are not met. Alex does not work 24 hours or more per week and does not receive benefits in their own right, but the key issue is that they are over 16 and not in approved education or training, which is required for continued Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old child Alex. While the claimant meets the basic residency requirements and lives with their child, Alex is over the age of 16 and is not currently in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility for children aged 16-19 requires that they remain in approved education or training. Since this condition is not met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
